layers.name: GUIDE_mouth_set__split_into_mouth_parts
layers.cubismUse: Reference only. Split into mouth line, inner mouth, teeth, tongue, and open/closed shapes for the production PSD.

layers.name: GUIDE_eyes_detail__split_left_right
layers.cubismUse: Reference only. Split into left/right eye whites, irises, pupils, highlights, upper lashes, lower lashes, and brows.

layers.name: GUIDE_ear_pair__split_left_right
layers.cubismUse: Reference only. Split into left/right outer ears and inner fur layers before Cubism mesh work.

layers.name: GUIDE_fox_mask__accessory_reference
layers.cubismUse: Accessory reference. Use as a separate fox mask ArtMesh once the production body PSD is split.

layers.name: GUIDE_ponytail__hair_reference
layers.cubismUse: Hair reference. Use as ponytail source for sway deformers after manual layer cleanup.

layers.name: RIN_bust_front__composite_placeholder
layers.cubismUse: Visible interim body layer. Replace with split head, face, hair, clothes, hands, and accessory layers for final Cubism authoring.

layers.name: RIN_tail_back__artmesh_source
layers.cubismUse: Visible interim tail layer. Split base fur and emerald tip before final tail physics setup.

layers.name: REF_front_body_no_tail__hidden
layers.cubismUse: Hidden full-body reference without tail for manual redraw and layer alignment.

layers.name: REF_front_fullbody__hidden
layers.cubismUse: Hidden full-body reference for proportions, outfit placement, and tail alignment.

cubismReadiness.reason: This PSD organizes the current cutout assets for Cubism handoff, but it is not a final ArtMesh-ready PSD. The composite bust still needs manual separation into facial, hair, clothing, and accessory parts.
requiredManualWork: Split bust composite into clean head, face, eye, brow, mouth, hair, clothes, hand, charm, and mask layers.
Separate left/right paired parts for symmetric parameters.
Redraw hidden areas needed for head turns and mouth/eye deformation.
Import the cleaned PSD into Live2D Cubism Editor and build ArtMeshes, deformers, physics, expressions, and motions.
